column2: A DNS {{c1::A}} record associates a host name with an IPv4 address.
column4:
Exam Trap

The record name is the letter A; the four-letter AAAA record serves IPv6.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.11; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Cloze HighYield DNS IPv4 Source::Messer-v170

column2: A DNS {{c1::AAAA}} record associates a host name with an IPv6 address.
column4:
Exam Trap

A and AAAA records perform parallel jobs for different IP versions.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.11; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Cloze HighYield DNS IPv6 Source::Messer-v170

column2: A DNS {{c1::CNAME}} record makes one name an alias of another host name.
column3: The alias follows the canonical name rather than storing a separate IP address.
column4:
Why

CNAME is useful when multiple service names should resolve through one canonical host name.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.11; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Cloze DNS Alias Source::Messer-v170

column2: A DNS {{c1::MX}} record identifies the host name of a domain's mail exchanger.
column3: The named mail host requires its own address record for IP resolution.
column4:
Exam Trap

An MX record points to a host name, not directly to the mail server's IP address.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.12; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Cloze HighYield DNS Email Source::Messer-v170

column2: DNS {{c1::TXT}} records publish text used for purposes such as domain verification and email-security information.
column3: SPF, DKIM, and DMARC information can be published through TXT records.
column4:
Why

TXT is a flexible container. The meaning comes from the structured information published in the record.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.12; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Cloze DNS TXT Source::Messer-v170
